description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a smooth finish and a visible grain pattern. The handle is ergonomically shaped, tapering at the end and featuring a hole, likely for hanging or attaching a lanyard. The axe head is made of metal, with a broad, flat poll and a single, wide cutting edge that is beveled for chopping. The blade is securely mounted onto the handle, with the metal extending through the top and appearing tightly fitted. The metal head has a matte, slightly weathered appearance, suggesting it is forged steel. No visible markings, engravings, or brand labels are present on either the handle or the blade.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a wide, single-edged metal blade, designed for chopping tasks. The handle is smooth and ergonomic, while the blade is robust and securely mounted.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from an angle highlighting the wide metal blade and the ergonomic curve of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the length and curvature of the handle, as well as the thickness of the metal head.
description_view_2: The side profile shows the full length of the handle and the rectangular shape of the axe head.
description_view_3: The handle's end with a hanging hole is visible, and the blade's beveled edge is clearly seen.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth finish, ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and a hanging hole at the end.
attribute_blade_design: Single wide beveled edge, rectangular blade with a flat poll, no beard, and a robust chopping profile.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle top.
